items.rationale: Lowering prover overhead unlocks far more applications and lets blockchains do more work without sacrificing decentralization; seen as endgame for scalability and verifiable compute.
items.age_note: Building through 2023 (Lasso/Jolt announced 2023-08), intensifying into 2024 — current and rising

citations.quote: Today we're releasing Jolt Alpha. Fastest zkVM in the world. Best developer experience. 100% open source and a public good.
as you bring that Pro overhead down the number of applicable applications for snarks grows dramatically

items.rationale: Applies decentralization to physical-world networks that are currently regulated monopolies; viewed as the likely face of mainstream consumer crypto.
items.age_note: Interest from 2023 (physical infra tweets), crystallized 2024 with Daylight Energy Series A and definitional tweets

citations.quote: leading the $9M Series A round in @daylightenergy! Daylight is building a decentralized protocol to fund and coordinate distributed energy resources.
The future of consumer crypto? DePIN

items.rationale: Crypto is a counterbalance to centralized AI: permissionless compute markets, proof-of-authenticity for AI content, and an IP/coordination layer for an explosion of AI-generated content.
items.age_note: Gensyn (2023-06) early; broadened in 2024 to provenance + Story Protocol IP layer

citations.quote: We need an open, global coordination layer in order for AI generated content to thrive. And this is exactly what blockchains can provide.
leading an $80M Series B financing of PIP Labs, developer of @storyprotocol... infrastructure for AI systems and creative people to happily co-exist.

items.rationale: Cheap, instant, global settlement that cuts out middlemen; seen as the on-ramp to bring billions of people on-chain once the efficiency-vs-reach tradeoff tips.
items.age_note: Long-standing goal restated strongly in 2024 ('pieces finally coming together')

citations.quote: Stablecoin payments could be the answer to how we bring billions of people onchain.
Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together.

items.rationale: Rollup-centric roadmap plus restaking (EigenLayer) and shared sequencing (Espresso) extend Ethereum's capabilities and fix rollup fragmentation; non-native DA needed for real scale.
items.age_note: Consistent 2023→2024; capital deployed in 2024 (EigenLayer Series B Feb, Espresso Series B Mar)

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges
Non-native DA is clearly required to scale Ethereum to its full potential in the short term.

items.rationale: Programmable on-chain IP/licensing lets creators capture downstream value and enables composable, community-owned brands — purely additive value creation.
items.age_note: IP-as-brand thesis from 2023, scaled with Story Protocol Series B in Aug 2024

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol... a programmable IP layer for the Internet
Creator royalties are a key value proposition of NFTs, but they've faced significant implementation challenges.

items.rationale: Lets networks recruit contributors with exogenous real-world incentives (e.g. ESPN reporting scores) and is seen as critical to onboarding a billion users.
items.age_note: Newly emphasized mid-2024; treated as underrated/early

citations.quote: I'm now convinced that web proofs/zkTLS will play a critical role in getting us there. It's all about incentives.
zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto.

items.rationale: Composable, player-owned game assets; games repeatedly drive software innovation and now top throughput charts (Proof of Play).
items.age_note: Investments through 2023 (Proof of Play, CCP); reaffirmed Aug 2024

citations.quote: #1 chain measured by Mgas/s (the correct measure for throughput) is @ProofOfPlay — by a long shot.

items.rationale: Open protocols give users portable identity and data, removing platform lock-in and deplatforming risk; competition powered by composability.
items.age_note: Persistent theme 2023–2024; listed among top 2024 excitements

citations.quote: I'm excited about DePIN, payments, and new social networks

items.rationale: Pure speculation deters quality founders, arms politicized regulators, and shapes how public/regulators see crypto — actively setting the industry back.
items.age_note: Sharply escalated in 2024 (Solana memecoin wave); the dominant 2024 concern

citations.quote: we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect.
Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.

items.rationale: Regulation-by-enforcement and stretched Howey theories foster uncertainty, de-banking, and offshoring; seen as a political agenda threatening U.S. leadership and national security.
items.age_note: Continuous 2023–2024; intensified mid-2024 with OpenSea Wells notice and shifting cases

citations.quote: The regulation-by-enforcement, the fostering of uncertainty, the de-banking, and the stretching of rules beyond comprehension has to end
This is a brazen example of the SEC's use of lawfare to chill innovation.

items.rationale: Hand-written circuits and complex zkVM codebases are riddled with bugs; today's systems are protected by obscurity, not by their security property — the biggest threat to SNARKs.
items.age_note: Smart-contract insecurity flagged 2023; ZK-specific concern sharpened 2024

citations.quote: they need to be secure they're not now they're riddled with bugs
the security issues... are the biggest threat to snarks not fulfilling their potential

items.rationale: Fixed/non-dilutable allocations and short lockups misalign incentives, enable VC dumping on retail, and jeopardize future fundraising and project longevity.
items.age_note: Raised late 2023, hammered repeatedly through 2024

citations.quote: We're seeing more and more crypto startups stuck with predatory terms from early-stage deals.
4 year/1 year cliff token lockups should be standard.

items.rationale: Without nuanced regulation, privacy protocols risk whole-pool sanctions; if crypto privacy becomes synonymous with money laundering, mainstream adoption is impossible.
items.age_note: Tornado Cash concerns 2023; restated 2024 re Samourai

citations.quote: if crypto privacy becomes synonymous with infinite money laundering/terrorist financing/sanctions avoidance we'll never reach critical mass.

items.rationale: During stress, liquidity providers withdraw first and markets flee to perceived-safe stablecoins, amplifying run risk on others; AMM curves aggravate draining in volatility.
items.age_note: Detailed in 2023-09 research; not prominently restated since, but unreversed

citations.quote: the flight to safety of the market triggers increased run risks on this kind of other stable coins that the market perceived to be less risky

items.rationale: Reordering and sniping extract value from users; harmful MEV extraction is a 'tax on using Ethereum' that better designs and faster blocks should mitigate.
items.age_note: Heavily discussed 2023; less prominent in 2024 statements but stance unchanged

citations.quote: Mev at uh at lunch which is also kind of like a tax on using ethereum

items.rationale: A single staking entity controlling >1/3 of stake concentrates governance and systemic risk.
items.age_note: Raised 2023-10; not restated in 2024

citations.quote: 8 million out of 22.7 million e... Stak are currently with Lio... controlling basically more than a third uh of the stake

items.rationale: A sufficiently large quantum computer could forge signatures and fork PoS; post-quantum signatures are large/slow, worsening state growth. Defenses known but a ~15-year watch item.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-06; long-horizon, not recently restated

citations.quote: the quantum addresser sign did a threat for blockchains but we know how to defend them

items.rationale: Rich developer ecosystem and composability; rollup-centric scaling roadmap maturing; regulatory beachhead established (treated as a commodity, not a security) opens path for all decentralized projects.
items.age_note: Developer-ecosystem thesis from 2023; ETF/non-security recognition reinforced bullishness in 2024

citations.quote: These approvals enshrine the SEC's position in 2018 - ETH is not a security... decentralization moves tokens outside of the US securities law regime.
Continued drumbeat of L2 progress in terms of reducing Ethereum transaction fees and increasing throughput

items.rationale: Cryptographically enforced scarcity makes it technically a better gold; deep community and long-proven security; potential to become programmable via a SNARK-verify opcode.
items.age_note: Stated 2023; not strongly restated in 2024 but unreversed

citations.quote: Bitcoin is just evidently a better version of gold than gold is... Bitcoin exploits cryptography and Mathematics to enforce that scarcity
adding a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to the bitcoin protocol — would make bitcoin totally programmable and free to move all over the internet.

items.rationale: Technically uninteresting, no underlying product/protocol/art; purely speculative and damaging to crypto's reputation, adoption, and regulatory standing.
items.age_note: Concern peaked in 2024

citations.quote: memecoins aren't very technically interesting. It shouldn't be a surprise that they're not attractive to builders.

items.rationale: Depend on market belief in created tokens; prone to death spirals (Terra). Regulators in US/EU now align against algorithmic designs.
items.age_note: Bearish/effectively dead since Terra collapse; SEC win over Terraform Dec 2023 confirmed stance

citations.quote: The SEC's victory over Terraform Labs today shouldn't surprise anyone.
as we've seen in the context of Terra... these things could lead very quickly to death Spirals and unravel

items.rationale: Historic reserve-quality and transparency concerns; flight-to-safety can flow toward it on regulatory risk but away from it on reserve-quality fears.
items.age_note: Stated 2023-09; not restated in 2024

citations.quote: there were a lot of concerns about the quality of the assets of tether and which also was particularly not transparent about the quality of these assets

risk_regime.rationale: The house is structurally bullish on building: it repeatedly cites the State of Crypto Index at all-time highs, record mobile-wallet users, DEX volumes, and maturing infra (ZK, L2s, restaking, DePIN, payments) as evidence the technology is crossing adoption thresholds. But the risk-on posture is explicitly long-term and builder-oriented — paired with strong distaste for the speculative 'casino' (memecoins) and acute concern about U.S. regulatory hostility. So: constructive/accumulate on fundamentals and infrastructure, dismissive of short-term speculative froth.

notes: Corpus is dominated by a16z crypto voices spanning research (Boneh, Thaler, Roughgarden, academic talks), policy/legal (Jennings, Quintenz on SEC), investing/thesis (Dixon, Lazzarin, Chitra/infra), and strategy (Skominers on competition/IP). Recency weighting: 2024 statements (ZK/Jolt, DePIN/Daylight, Story/AI-IP, stablecoin payments, anti-memecoin, anti-SEC-lawfare) were weighted over 2023. Notable continuity rather than reversal — most 2023 theses (decentralization, tokens-as-coordination-not-fundraising, regulate-apps-not-protocols, scaling) persisted and deepened into 2024. Few outright reversals; the clearest evolution is escalating, more pointed criticism of speculation/memecoins in 2024 vs. milder 2023 framing. Tokens are rarely named individually — the team discusses sectors and design principles far more than specific token calls, so token lists are necessarily thinner and partly inferred from sector/regulatory commentary. Solana/Base appear only as neutral adoption data points (Apr 2024), not as conviction calls. ETH-as-not-a-security and the ETF approval are treated as regulatory/structural wins rather than price calls.
